name: Vitamin D3 + K2 (MK-7)
purpose: Testosterone / DHT support via Leydig cell STAR protein; immune modulation; calcium routing
mechanism: D3 is a hormone signal, not just a bone vitamin. Activates the STAR protein in Leydig cells which facilitates cholesterol transport into mitochondria (the first step of steroidogenesis). VDR (vitamin D receptors) are expressed in follicle stem cells — VDR knockout mice develop alopecia. D3 supplementation over 1 year (3,332 IU/day) raised testosterone ~25% and free T ~20% in deficient men — with no effect in men already replete. 86% of men with pattern hair loss are vitamin D deficient. K2 MK-7 routes calcium properly (bones/teeth not arteries).
timing: Morning with food (fat-soluble — requires dietary fat for absorption)
cautions: Test 25(OH)D before dosing. No benefit in men already replete at 40–60 ng/mL.
Always pair D3 with K2 — high-dose D3 without K2 can cause soft tissue calcium deposition.
Pair with PTH and calcium for full picture.

name: Magnesium (Citrate / Glycinate / Bisglycinate / Threonate)
purpose: Sleep quality; cortisol regulation; testosterone support; thyroid conversion; steroidogenesis cofactor
mechanism: Involved in 300+ enzyme reactions. Inhibitory neurotransmitter support (GABA pathway) for sleep. Muscle relaxation. Lowers cortisol during prolonged stress — magnesium depleted quickly by chronic stress creating symptoms of hypothyroidism and hypogonadism. Required for steroidogenic enzyme activity throughout the pathway. Supports T4→T3 conversion. Binds to 5AR as a cofactor. Sleep dose targets falling asleep and staying asleep. Author personally uses 2–3g/day split across multiple forms.
dose: Sleep dose: 300–500mg 30–60 min before bed. General: 500mg–3g/day split in multiple doses. Start low.
timing: 30–60 min before bed (sleep); split across day (general hormonal support)
cautions: Excess GI discomfort (especially citrate — lower forms if this occurs).
Forms differ significantly in absorption and effect: Glycinate/bisglycinate = most bioavailable/gentle; Threonate = best brain penetration; Citrate = good general form but laxative at high doses.
Boron can cause waking after 6–7h — not a magnesium caution but important sleep context.

name: Zinc (Picolinate / Bisglycinate / Citrate / Gluconate)
purpose: 5AR cofactor; testosterone synthesis; immune function; DHT production
mechanism: Zinc is a direct cofactor for steroidogenic enzymes including 5-alpha-reductase and 17β-HSD. Zinc restriction over 20 weeks cut testosterone significantly in healthy young men; 30mg/day in deficient elderly men raised it substantially. Required for the proper function of multiple enzymes in the steroidogenesis pathway. Zinc deficiency directly limits testosterone production. Note: must be balanced with copper — long-term high-dose zinc depletes copper. Do NOT take zinc oxide form (poorly absorbed).
timing: With food to reduce nausea; not at same time as high-phytate foods
cautions: Always balance with copper — long-term zinc supplementation without copper monitoring can deplete copper, leading to anemia and neurological issues.
Zinc oxide form poorly absorbed — avoid.
Monitor serum zinc and copper together. Target zinc 100–120 µg/dL; copper 90–120 µg/dL.

name: Selenium
purpose: Deiodinase function (T4→T3 conversion); Leydig cell protection
mechanism: Selenium is a cofactor for all three deiodinase enzymes (DIO1, DIO2, DIO3) that convert T4 to active T3. DIO1 specifically converts T4→T3 and clears reverse T3 — it is killed by selenium deficiency. Also protects Leydig cells from oxidative damage. Required for thyroid peroxidase (TPO) function. Above 140 ng/mL provides no additional gain and may increase pro-oxidant risk.
cautions: Do not exceed 200 mcg/day — above 140 ng/mL serum is pro-oxidant territory.
Selenomethionine preferred over selenite for absorption.
Test serum selenium — optimal 100–130 ng/mL.

name: Creatine Monohydrate (Creapure grade preferred)
purpose: ATP regeneration; NADPH support for 5AR; possible direct DHT bump; strength/muscle
mechanism: Creatine regenerates ATP via the phosphocreatine system — the fastest energy pathway. Also supports NADPH availability as a cofactor for 5-alpha-reductase. Some evidence of a direct DHT bump, likely via increased testosterone→DHT conversion efficiency when cellular energy is high. Creapure grade recommended for purity. Author uses 10–15g/day split across meals.
timing: Split across meals; timing flexibility is high
cautions: Slight water retention in early weeks is normal.
No clinically established cautions at standard doses in healthy individuals per KB.
Creapure grade preferred to avoid impurities.

name: Boron (Glycinate / Citrate / Calcium Fructoborate)
purpose: Lowers SHBG; raises free testosterone and DHT; reduces hsCRP/TNF-α
mechanism: Boron suppresses sex hormone binding globulin (SHBG), which binds testosterone and DHT tightly. Lower SHBG = more free testosterone available as substrate for 5AR → more DHT. Also reduces inflammatory cytokines hsCRP and TNF-α (which epigenetically silence 5AR). Buy citrate or glycinate, NOT boric acid.
timing: Morning with food. NOTE: Boron can disrupt sleep if taken later in the day (causes waking after 6–7h feeling alert). Take in the morning only.
cautions: Take in the morning ONLY — causes early waking if taken in afternoon/evening (author's personal experience).
Buy glycinate or citrate form, not boric acid.
Do not exceed 10mg/day beyond short-term experiments.

name: Vitamin C
purpose: Leydig cell protection; cortisol reduction; collagen synthesis
mechanism: Leydig cells are among the most antioxidant-rich cells in the body; vitamin C is a primary antioxidant protecting them from oxidative damage during steroidogenesis. Also reduces cortisol output under stress by supporting adrenal function. Required for collagen synthesis (take with collagen for maximum benefit — Hair Loss protocol specifies 1g Vitamin C with collagen supplements). Note: do NOT take vitamin C at night (can cause sleep disruption per author's experience).
timing: Daytime only — NOT at night (can cause sleep disruption). Split doses.
cautions: Do not take at night — author experienced sleep disruption.
Very high doses may cause GI discomfort (loose stool) — start lower and work up.
Bowel tolerance varies significantly.

name: B-Complex (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, Folate)
purpose: NADPH production via pentose phosphate pathway; steroidogenesis; thyroid T4→T3 conversion
mechanism: B-vitamins are cofactors for the pentose phosphate pathway (PPP) which generates NADPH — the rate-limiting cofactor for 5-alpha-reductase. Without NADPH, testosterone cannot be converted to DHT regardless of T levels. B1 (thiamine), B3 (niacin/niacinamide), and B6 are the most critical. B6 caution: avoid daily high-dose B6 — neuropathy risk. Author recommends P5P form at 30mg, 2× per week maximum.
dose: Standard B-complex daily. B6 as P5P: 30mg maximum, 2× per week (NOT daily).
cautions: B6 peripheral neuropathy risk with daily high-dose supplementation — use P5P form at 30mg maximum, 2× per week only.
Avoid B6 (pyridoxine HCl) in high daily doses.
B12 and folate interact — supplement balanced forms.

name: Omega-3 (EPA + DHA Fish Oil)
purpose: Anti-inflammatory; vascular health; mood; note: DHA inhibits 5AR
mechanism: EPA reduces systemic inflammation (lowers hsCRP, IL-6, TNF-α) which indirectly supports testosterone and DHT by reducing epigenetic silencing of SRD5A2. IMPORTANT NUANCE: DHA is in the ranking of fatty acid 5AR inhibitors (GLA > DHA > Arachidonic acid > ALA > Linoleic acid). DHA inhibits 5AR at high doses. For DHT-focused goals, use in moderation. Keep fish oil cold (PUFA oxidizes rapidly at room temperature) — store in fridge.
cautions: DHA inhibits 5-alpha-reductase — use in moderation if DHT is a primary goal.
Store in refrigerator — PUFA oxidizes rapidly at room temperature, creating the exact lipid peroxidation you're trying to avoid.
Triglyceride form preferred over ethyl ester for absorption.

name: Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma longifolia) — Standardized Extract
purpose: Lowers cortisol; nudges LH and free testosterone upward
mechanism: Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma longifolia) is the most evidence-backed natural testosterone support. Works primarily by lowering cortisol (freeing cholesterol substrate for testosterone) and modestly nudging LH upward. Some evidence of direct Leydig cell support. Must use standardized extract — non-standardized products are unreliable. Evidence-based among the adaptogenic compounds.
timing: Morning or split; cycles 5–8 weeks on, 2–4 weeks off
cautions: Use standardized extract only — generic products have variable potency.
Cycle: 5–8 weeks on, 2–4 weeks off.
Not for use with existing hormone therapy without medical supervision.

name: Ashwagandha (KSM-66 or Sensoril)
purpose: Stress and cortisol reduction; short-term use for acute stress seasons
mechanism: Adaptogenic herb that reduces cortisol and anxiety, supporting the HPG axis by reducing cortisol-driven pregnenolone steal. Short-term use during seasons of high stress is the recommendation. Author discontinued after experiencing emotional blunting ('numb to emotions'). Not recommended for long-term continuous use.
timing: Morning or evening; short-term cycles only
cautions: Author discontinued due to emotional blunting — 'I felt numb to emotions.'
Recommend for seasons of stress then stopping — NOT long-term continuous.
Some people experience thyroid interactions — monitor if on thyroid medication.

name: L-Theanine
purpose: Sleep: promotes relaxation, reduces anxiety without sedation; pre-bed calming
mechanism: Promotes alpha brain wave activity and GABAergic tone without causing sedation. Reduces anxiety. May cause vivid dreams at higher doses. Part of author's core sleep stack. Also used short-term for stress management.
timing: 30 min before bed (sleep); can also be taken during day for stress reduction
cautions: May cause vivid dreams — lower dose if this occurs.
Generally very well tolerated.

name: Apigenin
purpose: Sleep calming; mild estrogen suppressor; 3α-HSD inhibitor (slows DHT breakdown)
mechanism: Flavonoid that binds brain receptors involved in sleep/calming (GABAergic activity). Huberman notes it is a mild estrogen suppressor — 'not recommended for women.' Also acts as a 3α-HSD inhibitor, slowing DHT breakdown into weaker metabolites. Part of author's core sleep stack. Minimum starting point if overwhelmed by full sleep stack.
cautions: Huberman notes mild estrogen suppressor activity — use carefully in men, particularly those with already low estrogen.
Part of minimum starter sleep stack (Mg + L-Theanine + Apigenin).

name: Valerian Root
purpose: Calming; sedative properties for sleep
mechanism: Sedative and calming effects. Included in author's full sleep stack.
cautions: Part of the fuller sleep stack — not required in starter stack.
Some individuals experience vivid dreams or GI sensitivity.

name: Passion Flower (Passiflora incarnata)
purpose: Sleep quality; calming nervous system
mechanism: Author: 'Tremendously helped my sleep.' Calming effect on nervous system. Part of full sleep stack.
dose: 200–400mg (up to 500mg per some sources in KB)
cautions: Part of the fuller sleep stack.
Generally well tolerated.

name: Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis)
purpose: Calms nervous system; reduces nighttime awakenings
mechanism: Calms nervous system; reduces nighttime awakenings per ebook. Part of full sleep stack.
cautions: Part of fuller sleep stack.

name: Tart Cherry Extract
purpose: Naturally increases melatonin precursors; anthocyanins, tryptophan
mechanism: 'Doesn't affect the body the same way as actually taking melatonin.' Contains natural tryptophan and anthocyanins that support melatonin pathway without the direct hormone effects of melatonin supplementation.
cautions: Author is against melatonin supplementation but considers tart cherry a safer alternative.
Not part of author's core stack.

name: Glycine
purpose: Sleep: decreases core body temperature; promotes deeper sleep. Also joint / collagen support.
mechanism: Glycine decreases core body temperature by dilating peripheral blood vessels. Lower core temp is required for deep N3 sleep. Also structural amino acid in collagen. Per Huberman's protocol: 2g taken 2–3 times per week (not nightly). Author experienced racing thoughts at higher use — individual response varies.
dose: 2g (2–3 times per week per Huberman protocol); some sources list 3–5g/day for general use
timing: Pre-bed for sleep; anytime for collagen/joint support
cautions: Author experienced racing thoughts at some dosing — individual response varies.
Per Huberman: 2–3 times per week, not every night.

name: CBD Oil
purpose: Sleep support; pre-bed calming
mechanism: Author uses 7 drops of 2% CBD oil taken 5 hours before bed — the longest lead time in his sleep stack. Mechanism not elaborated beyond calming effect in the KB.
cautions: Individual response varies significantly.
Not part of required sleep stack — optional.
Author notes effectiveness but dosing is personal.

name: Fadogia Agrestis
purpose: Experimental LH/testosterone support — limited human data
mechanism: Proposed to stimulate LH release and support testosterone. Primarily animal data. Limited robust human RCT evidence. Author uses in 4–6 week cycles.
timing: Cycles: 4–6 weeks on, rest period; not for continuous use
cautions: Primarily animal data — limited human RCT evidence.
4–6 week cycles only.
Hepatotoxicity concerns noted in animal literature — caution at high doses.
Author marks as experimental.

name: L-Carnitine L-Tartrate (LCLT)
purpose: Fat transport; CoA management; androgen receptor density; dual-fuel activation
mechanism: Carnitine frees CoA from fat oxidation so CoA is available for glucose oxidation simultaneously — 'with carnitine, fat burns, carbs burn, neither one is waiting in line.' Also increases androgen receptor (AR) density in muscle — more receptors = more response to same DHT/T. LCLT is the preferred form for post-workout use (taken with carbs and protein for absorption). L-Tartrate form provides fast absorption.
dose: 2–3g pre-workout or 2g post-workout with carbs + protein
timing: Pre-workout (2–3g for performance); or post-workout with carbs and protein
cautions: Take with carbs and protein for best absorption.
LCLT form preferred over L-carnitine base for bioavailability.

name: R-ALA (R-Alpha Lipoic Acid)
purpose: PDH activation (glucose→mitochondria routing); insulin sensitivity; AMPK; antioxidant recycler
mechanism: R-ALA activates pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH) — the gate for carbohydrates entering the mitochondrial energy pathway. Training activates PDH through calcium; R-ALA activates it through a separate mechanism — 'gate opens wider, stays open longer.' Activates AMPK (mitochondrial biogenesis signal). Also a powerful antioxidant that recycles other antioxidants (glutathione, vitamin C, vitamin E). Improves insulin sensitivity and glucose disposal post-workout. R form has much higher bioavailability than S or racemic ALA.
cautions: R-form only — racemic or S-ALA is significantly less effective.
May lower blood sugar substantially — do not take fasted if prone to hypoglycemia.
Take with post-workout carbs.

name: HMB (β-Hydroxy β-Methylbutyrate)
purpose: mTOR + AMPK dual activation; anti-catabolic; muscle preservation
mechanism: HMB activates mTOR (anabolism/protein synthesis via PI3K/Akt pathway) AND AMPK (mitochondrial biogenesis via LKB1 pathway) simultaneously through non-competing pathways — 'two separate molecular doors; neither cancels the other.' Most compounds force a tradeoff between growth and adaptation. HMB is unusual in triggering both. Anti-catabolic: reduces protein breakdown during training.
timing: Split across day; one dose post-workout
cautions: Free acid form preferred over calcium HMB for faster absorption.
Benefits most pronounced in trained individuals during caloric restriction.

name: Taurine
purpose: Cell swelling (anabolic signal); GABA-A (parasympathetic); bile acid/T4→T3 tissue signaling
mechanism: Taurine osmotically pulls water into cells → cell swelling → mechanosensitive mTOR activation (independent of nutrition signals). Also acts on GABA-A receptors promoting parasympathetic tone (important for post-workout recovery/digestion state). Post-workout: activates TGR5 receptor in muscle via conjugated bile acids → stimulates local T4→T3 conversion at tissue level exactly when metabolic demand is highest. 'Taurine is quietly supporting thyroid activation at the tissue level when metabolic demand for active thyroid hormone is highest.'
timing: Post-workout (key); can split remainder across day
cautions: Generally very well tolerated.
Distinct from energy drink taurine context — doses here are therapeutic, not stimulant.

name: Kestose (1-kestose ≥90% purity)
purpose: Feeds gut Bacteroidota expressing 5AR genes → local testosterone→DHT conversion in gut
mechanism: Gut fecal free DHT in healthy young men is >70× serum DHT. Bacteroidota bacteria express 5AR genes and convert testosterone to DHT locally in the GI tract. Kestose (a specific prebiotic fructooligosaccharide) selectively feeds Bacteroidota. Must be 1-kestose ≥90% purity — NOT generic FOS which may feed less favorable bacteria. Start low to allow GI adaptation.
dose: 5g 2× per day (start at 2–3g 2× per day for first week to allow GI adaptation)
cautions: Must be 1-kestose ≥90% purity — generic FOS is NOT equivalent.
Start at 2–3g 2×/day for first week to allow GI adaptation.
GI gas/loose stool is common early — reduce dose and build slowly.
Part of DHT Starter Stack Protocol — introduce in Week 2 after establishing nutrition baseline.

name: Topical DHEA (Scrotal Application)
purpose: Backdoor DHT production pathway via scrotal 5AR-rich skin
mechanism: Scrotal skin has high 5AR expression — applying DHEA topically here converts locally to DHT with high efficiency. Small dose has large local effect. Backdoor pathway: DHEA → androstenedione → DHT without going through serum testosterone. ONLY for men not prone to aromatization. Start at 5–10mg; increase to 10–20mg only after 2 weeks with no estrogen signs.
dose: Start 5–10mg; can raise to 10–20mg after 2 weeks only if no estrogen signs
cautions: Advanced protocol — only after establishing nutrition/sleep/training baseline (first 3 weeks of DHT Starter Stack).
ONLY if not prone to aromatization — watch for water retention, soft look, nipple sensitivity, mood swings.
If any estrogen signs appear: drop DHEA immediately.
Red flags to pause: water retention, soft look, nipple sensitivity, mood swings.
Monitor E2 risk.

name: Lactobacillus reuteri (Strains 6475 + 17938)
purpose: Gut oxytocin production → 5AR (SRD5A) upregulation; DHT support via gut-oxytocin axis
mechanism: L. reuteri strains 6475 and 17938 stimulate oxytocin production in the gut. Oxytocin increased 5AR (SRD5A) activity in both isoforms I and II and raised prostatic DHT in controlled rat studies. Also increased 5AR in human prostate stromal/epithelial cells in vitro. These specific strains matter — not interchangeable with generic L. reuteri.
dose: Not clearly stated in KB — use standard probiotic dosing for these specific strains
timing: With food or as directed on product
cautions: Must use strains 6475 AND 17938 specifically — not generic L. reuteri.
Evidence is from animal/in vitro studies for DHT pathway specifically.

name: Sodium Butyrate (Butyrate)
purpose: HDAC inhibitor → reverses 5AR gene methylation; gut barrier support
mechanism: Butyrate is an HDAC (histone deacetylase) inhibitor. HDAC inhibition can reverse the epigenetic silencing of the SRD5A2 promoter caused by chronic inflammation (TNF-α/IL-6 → DNMT1 → methylation). This is one of the tools to restore 5AR expression in men with stubbornly low DHT despite normal T/T3/IGF-1. Also feeds colonocytes and supports gut barrier integrity.
dose: Not clearly stated in KB — standard gut protocol dosing
cautions: Strong smell/taste — enteric-coated forms preferred.
Part of gut-5AR restoration protocol.

name: Ubiquinol (CoQ10 reduced form)
purpose: Mitochondrial electron transport; cellular energy; antioxidant
mechanism: Ubiquinol is the reduced (active) form of CoQ10. Required in mitochondrial electron transport chain. Supports cellular ATP production. Part of the mitochondria stack in TFF 2.0.
cautions: Ubiquinol form preferred over ubiquinone for absorption.
Fat required for absorption.

name: NAD+ Precursors (NR or NMN)
purpose: NAD+ replenishment; mitochondrial function; cellular energy metabolism
mechanism: NAD+ is required for mitochondrial function and is a component of NADPH (the 5AR cofactor). NR (nicotinamide riboside) and NMN (nicotinamide mononucleotide) both raise NAD+ levels. Part of mitochondria stack.
cautions: NR and NMN have different conversion pathways — not clearly compared in KB.
Part of optional mitochondria stack.

name: PQQ (Pyrroloquinoline Quinone)
purpose: Mitochondrial biogenesis; cellular energy
mechanism: Supports mitochondrial biogenesis (creation of new mitochondria). Part of the mitochondria stack in TFF 2.0.
cautions: Part of optional mitochondria stack.

name: Myo-Inositol
purpose: Sleep: may help falling back asleep after waking
mechanism: Per Huberman protocol. May help falling back asleep after nighttime wakings. Author experienced racing thoughts once — individual response varies. Dose ranges widely (500mg–5g).
timing: Before bed or upon waking during night
cautions: Individual response varies significantly.
Author experienced racing thoughts — try lower end first.

name: Collagen Peptides / Bone Broth Concentrate
purpose: Hair keratin synthesis; joint support; gut lining support
mechanism: Hair is made of keratin which requires glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline — amino acids sparse in muscle meat but abundant in collagen. 10–20g/day recommended. Take with 1g Vitamin C (required for collagen synthesis — vitamin C is the cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase which produces hydroxyproline). Muscle meat alone will not provide adequate glycine/proline for follicle keratin demands.
timing: Any time; take with 1g Vitamin C
cautions: Take with Vitamin C (1g) for proper utilization.
Bone broth also a suitable source.

name: Joint Stack (MSM + Glucosamine + Chondroitin)
purpose: Joint support for heavy compound training
mechanism: Standard joint support stack for connective tissue maintenance during heavy compound lifting. MSM provides sulfur. Glucosamine and chondroitin support cartilage matrix.
dose: Not clearly stated in KB beyond being listed in training day checklist
cautions: Not clearly stated in KB.
source_refs: Not clearly stated in KB — listed in training day checklist section

name: GABA
purpose: Sleep: inhibitory neurotransmitter; promotes relaxation
mechanism: Author experienced waking with 'racing thoughts' and discontinued. Poor CNS penetration is a known limitation of oral GABA. Individual responses vary widely.
cautions: Author discontinued — experienced racing thoughts upon waking.
Poor blood-brain barrier penetration may limit efficacy.
Individual response highly variable.

name: L-Tryptophan / 5-HTP
purpose: Sleep: serotonin → melatonin precursor
mechanism: Both are precursors to serotonin, which converts to melatonin. 5-HTP is stronger (bypasses the initial conversion step). Author stopped L-Tryptophan as it caused early waking after 5–7h. Note: excessive serotonin can suppress thyroid and increase cortisol.
cautions: Author stopped L-Tryptophan due to early waking at 5–7h.
Excessive serotonin pathway stimulation can suppress thyroid and increase cortisol per hair loss ebook.
Do not combine with SSRIs.

name: Intra-Workout Glucose (Dextrose / Cyclic Dextrin)
purpose: Intra-workout glucose for glycolysis; GLUT4-independent muscle fuelling during training
mechanism: During training, muscle contractions open GLUT4 doors independently of insulin. Starting intra-workout glucose after the first 1–2 heavy sets provides direct fuel for glycolytic demand without the pre-workout insulin spike that would shut down lipolysis. 2g sodium added per serving for SGLT1 co-transporter uptake (sodium-glucose linked absorption). Dextrose (pure glucose) or cyclic dextrin (faster gastric emptying) are both suitable.
timing: Intra-workout — start after 1–2 hard sets (NOT before training starts)
cautions: Do NOT take pre-workout — pre-carb spikes insulin → shuts lipolysis → energy mismatch.
Start after 1–2 heavy sets only.
Add 2g sodium for proper SGLT1 uptake.
